author: gb9t
content:
Phase A part 2 written: .design/00-rust-conventions.md (365 lines, 17 REQs, 18 ACs, 2 OPEN questions remaining).

Key content:
- Aligned with rust-for-linux: edition 2021, MSRV 1.85.0, bindgen 0.71.1; tracks upstream not unilaterally
- no_std mandatory; alloc permitted but only via KBox/KVec with explicit GFP flags (no Box::new etc.)
- Panic-source forbidden list: panic!, unwrap, expect, unreachable!, todo!, unimplemented!, slice indexing, bare arithmetic
- Error model: kernel::error::Error newtype around NonZeroI32 (existing upstream); no per-subsystem error enums
- async/await/Future/.await/executors: forbidden
- Concurrency primitives table distinguishes upstream-existing (Mutex, SpinLock, Refcount, Atomic, Rcu, Completion, CondVar, SetOnce, Task, workqueue Work/DelayedWork/Queue, KBox/KVec) from Rookery-to-author (RwSemaphore, Kthread spawn, PerCpu)
- Tasklet abstraction NOT planned — upstream is migrating to BH workqueues (system_bh()); Rookery follows
- Verification stack — full Layer 1-4 detailed: Kani SAFETY proofs (mandatory all unsafe), TLA+ models (mandatory for concurrency primitives), Kani DS invariants (mandatory page_alloc/slab/dcache/runqueue), Creusot/Verus/Prusti (per-subsystem opt-in)
- kbuild integration; lints; rustfmt; rustdoc gates
- FFI confined to bindings/ or ffi.rs
- Forbidden patterns table with corresponding clippy lint names

Tracking issues spawned:
- #3 (high, blocking): Author Rookery-specific clippy lints (unsafe_without_proof, panic_in_kernel_path, async_in_kernel, alloc_without_gfp, bare_arithmetic_on_size, unstable_feature_without_decision)
- #4 (medium): Author missing rust-for-linux abstractions (RwSemaphore, Kthread, PerCpu)

Both blocked by #1.

Two open questions remain in the conventions doc:
- Q1: lint authoring schedule — block subsystem implementation on lints landing first?
- Q2: TLA+ tooling default — TLC vs Apalache?

All architecture references verified to resolve under /home/doll/linux-src.
